youtube.pageTitle: 如何下载 4K YouTube 视频 — 免费，无广告 | SSvid
youtube.pageDesc: 免费下载 4K、1080p 或 MP3 格式的 YouTube 视频。无广告、无水印、无追踪。支持 macOS、Windows 和 Linux。使用 SSvid 的分步指南。
youtube.subtitle: 以最高画质将任意 YouTube 视频保存到您的电脑 — 4K、1080p 或仅音频 MP3。免费，无广告，无水印，无需账号。
youtube.intro: YouTube doesn't offer a built-in download feature for desktop (only mobile Premium subscribers get offline access). SSvid fills that gap — a free desktop app that downloads any YouTube video in its original quality, with generous free downloads.
youtube.need1: SSvid — free download from ssvid.app (macOS, Windows, Linux)
youtube.need3: That's it. No account, no signup, no browser extension.
youtube.step1Desc: Go to ssvid.app and download the app for your platform. On macOS, drag to Applications. On Windows, run the installer. On Linux, make the AppImage executable and run it. Takes about 30 seconds.
youtube.step2Desc: Go to YouTube in your browser. Find the video you want. Copy the URL from your browser's address bar. It looks like: https://youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ
youtube.step3Desc: Open SSvid and paste the URL into the search bar at the top. SSvid automatically detects the video, extracts metadata (title, duration, thumbnail), and shows all available download options.
youtube.step4Desc: Pick your preferred resolution and format. SSvid shows the file size for each option so you know exactly what you're getting before downloading.
youtube.step5Desc: Click Download. SSvid's Rust engine handles the rest — fast parallel streams, automatic format merging, and pause/resume if your connection drops. The file saves to your chosen download folder.
youtube.tip: Tip: You can also paste YouTube playlist URLs to download entire playlists, or channel URLs to download recent uploads. SSvid handles up to 10 downloads at once.
youtube.feat1: Regular videos — any public or unlisted YouTube video
youtube.feat2: Playlists — paste a playlist URL to download all videos
youtube.feat3: Channels — download recent uploads from any channel
youtube.feat4: YouTube Shorts — download Shorts just like regular videos
youtube.feat5: Age-restricted videos — SSvid can download age-restricted content without logging in
youtube.feat7: 4K, HDR, 60fps — full support for high-quality formats
youtube.feat8: Audio extraction — extract audio as MP3 or M4A
youtube.h2_4: Why SSvid Instead of Online Converters?
youtube.whyIntro: Online YouTube converters (y2mate, savefrom, etc.) are slow, ad-filled, and often capped at 720p. Many inject malware or redirect to scam pages. SSvid is different:
youtube.why1: Full quality — 4K, 1080p, HDR. Online converters rarely go above 720p.
youtube.why2: No ads — zero popups, zero redirects, zero tracking.
youtube.why3: 10x faster — Rust download engine with parallel streams vs. single-thread web converters.
youtube.why4: Works offline — once installed, SSvid needs no browser. No website downtime affects you.
youtube.why5: Batch downloads — download entire playlists. Online converters do one video at a time.
youtube.why6: Pause & resume — lost connection? Pick up where you left off. Online converters start over.

guide.intro: If you've ever tried to save a video from YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, or any of the thousands of platforms on the web, you know the drill: a maze of online converters covered in pop-ups, browser extensions that break every other update, and "free" apps stuffed with bloatware. This guide cuts through the noise. It explains exactly how video downloading works, the four methods you can use today, when each one is the right choice, and our recommendation after testing every option.
guide.h2FourWays: The Four Ways to Download Videos in 2026
guide.fourWaysIntro: There are exactly four categories of tools. Every product on the market falls into one of them.
guide.onlineConvertersDesc: Sites like y2mate, savefrom, ssyoutube, and dozens of clones. You paste a URL, the server downloads the video, and you download it from the server. The convenience is real — no install — but the trade-offs are brutal.
guide.onlineConverters1: Quality is capped at 720p on most sites, regardless of what the source offers. The 4K, HDR, and 60fps streams require authenticated requests the converter can't make.
guide.onlineConverters2: Servers go down constantly as platforms patch the underlying APIs. Today's working site is tomorrow's dead link.
guide.onlineConverters3: Ads and malware risk — converters monetize through aggressive pop-ups and redirects. Many inject browser hijackers or push fake "update Flash" prompts.
guide.onlineConverters4: Server-side means non-private. The operator sees every URL you submit. Some log them.
guide.onlineConvertersUseIf: Use only if: you need one video, fast, and don't care about quality.
guide.browserExtensionsDesc: Chrome/Firefox extensions that detect video URLs in the page and offer a download button. They work in your browser context, so they can capture authenticated streams (e.g., logged-in YouTube). Quality is usually better than online converters.
guide.browserExtensions1: Constant maintenance — every site update breaks them. Most extensions go stale within weeks.
guide.browserExtensions2: YouTube blocks them aggressively. Chrome Web Store actively removes YouTube downloaders due to YouTube's policy. The good ones live on Firefox or self-hosted.
guide.browserExtensions3: Privacy concerns: extensions have access to every page you visit. Pick wrong and you've just installed spyware.
guide.browserExtensionsUseIf: Use only if: you download from one platform and trust the developer.
guide.cliToolsDesc: The open-source yt-dlp is the gold standard. It's a Python command-line tool that supports 1700+ sites, every format under the sun, and every quality the source offers. It's the engine behind almost every legitimate video downloader on the planet, including SSvid.
guide.cliTools1: Maximum capability: 4K, HDR, 60fps, age-restricted, private with cookies, playlists, channels, subtitles, chapters — everything.
guide.cliTools2: 100% private and local: nothing leaves your machine.
guide.cliTools3: Steep learning curve: you live in the terminal. Quality selection requires knowing format codes. Updating means `pip install -U yt-dlp` and hoping it doesn't break your system Python.
guide.cliTools4: No GUI: no progress bars beyond text, no thumbnails, no preview, no library.
guide.cliToolsUseIf: Use only if: you're comfortable with the terminal and want maximum control.
guide.desktopAppsDesc: A desktop app wraps the same yt-dlp engine in a native UI with a download manager, library, format selector, in-app browser, and built-in player. You get the maximum capability of the CLI without any of the friction.
guide.desktopApps1: Same engine as yt-dlp → same 1700+ sites, same 4K/HDR support, same private local downloads.
guide.desktopApps2: Native UI: paste URL, see thumbnail/title/duration instantly, pick quality from a dropdown, hit download. No commands to memorize.
guide.desktopApps3: Bundled binaries: yt-dlp and ffmpeg ship inside the app. No Python install, no PATH wrangling. Updates happen in-app.
guide.desktopApps4: Library and player: downloaded videos appear in a searchable library. Tap to play in the built-in media player with PiP. No need to fire up VLC.
guide.desktopApps5: Parallel downloads: queue 10 videos at once. The app handles concurrency, retries, and resume.
guide.desktopAppsUseIf: Use if: you want the maximum capability of yt-dlp with zero terminal time.
guide.recommendation: Our recommendation: If you download videos more than once a month, install a desktop app and forget the other three methods exist. We obviously think SSvid is the best — it's free, open-source engine, signed and notarized, native on Apple Silicon, and has no ads. But every desktop app built on yt-dlp will outperform every online converter and most extensions.

guide.platformYoutubeDesc: 4K, playlists, Shorts, age-restricted, audio only, subtitles.

guide.legalIntro: Video downloading sits in a grey area that depends on what you do with the file. In most jurisdictions, downloading a publicly-available video for personal, offline viewing is treated as fair use (US), private copying (EU), or simply not pursued. The line gets crossed when you:
guide.legal1: Redistribute the file — uploading to another platform, sharing publicly.
guide.legal2: Strip DRM — most subscription platforms (Netflix, Disney+, Spotify Premium) use DRM and removing it is illegal under DMCA / copyright directives.
guide.legal3: Use commercially without a license — using a downloaded video in a video you sell or run ads on.
guide.legal4: Violate the platform's ToS — most platforms prohibit downloads in their terms. ToS violations are not criminal but can get your account banned.
guide.legalOutro: SSvid doesn't bypass DRM. It downloads the same streams your browser plays — public videos anyone with a URL can access. What you do with the file is on you.
guide.privacyIntro: If you care about privacy, the order of preference is clear:
guide.privacy1: Local desktop apps — videos download directly from the source to your disk. Nothing touches a third party.
guide.privacy2: CLI tools — same as above, no telemetry.
guide.privacy3: Browser extensions — depends on the extension. Vet the developer.
guide.privacy4: Online converters — every URL you submit is visible to the converter operator. Some log them, some sell the logs, all are subpoena-able.
guide.qualityDesc1: Modern video platforms serve multiple quality tiers from the same URL — 144p, 240p, 360p, 480p, 720p, 1080p, 1440p, 2160p (4K), and sometimes 4320p (8K) or HDR variants. The downloader picks one. Online converters almost always pick the lowest-bandwidth option to save server costs. Desktop apps and CLI tools let you pick the best.
guide.qualityDesc2: For most people, 1080p is enough — it's the resolution of HD streaming services and matches most TVs and monitors. If you have a 4K display and care about archival quality, 4K downloads are bigger (~3-5x the file size of 1080p) but visibly sharper.
